equipment.description: Thick-walled cylindrical steel vessel containing the nuclear reactor core.
Large heat exchanger where primary coolant transfers heat to secondary coolant to produce steam.
Large vertical pump circulating primary coolant through the reactor core and steam generators.
Vessel used to control pressure in the primary coolant loop of a PWR.
Tank designed to condense and cool steam discharged from pressurizer relief valves.
High-pressure pump supplying makeup water to the primary coolant system.
Pump used to transfer concentrated boric acid for reactivity control.
Heat exchanger cooling primary coolant drawn off for purification.
Pump circulating coolant to remove decay heat after reactor shutdown.
Heat exchanger transferring decay heat from the RHR system to the component cooling water system.
Pump providing water to spray headers to reduce containment pressure during an accident.
High-pressure pump for emergency core cooling.
Pressurized tank providing rapid injection of borated water into the reactor core.
Tank providing surge capacity and surge gas control for the primary coolant.
Heat exchanger recovering heat from letdown flow to preheat charging flow.
Filter removing particulates from seal injection water for reactor coolant pumps.
Vessel containing ion exchange resin to remove impurities from reactor coolant.
Tank used for mixing and preparing boric acid solution.
Tank storing demineralized water for primary system makeup.
Large tank storing borated water for refueling operations and emergency core cooling.
Pump circulating water through the spent fuel pool and its heat exchangers.
Heat exchanger removing decay heat from spent fuel pool water.
Pump circulating cooling water to various nuclear components.
Heat exchanger transferring heat from the CCW system to the essential service water system.
Pump supplying cooling water from the ultimate heat sink to safety-related equipment.
Large, fast-acting valve to isolate the steam generator in the event of a steam line break.
Valve used to relieve pressure transients in the pressurizer.
Spring-loaded safety valve for overpressure protection of the primary system.
High-capacity pump supplying feedwater to the steam generators.
Pump providing emergency feedwater to steam generators if main feedwater is lost.
Pump removing condensate from the main condenser hotwell.
Large heat exchanger condensing turbine exhaust steam back into water.
Vessel removing moisture and reheating steam between high and low pressure turbines.
Heat exchanger preheating feedwater using extraction steam before it enters the steam generator.
Heat exchanger preheating condensate before it enters the feedwater pumps.
Steam turbine converting thermal energy into mechanical energy to drive the main generator.
Synchronous generator driven by the main turbine to produce electrical power.
Diesel-driven generator providing backup power to safety-related equipment.
Alternative AC power source during a complete loss of offsite and onsite emergency power.
Mechanism used to insert or withdraw control rods in the reactor core.
Detector located outside the reactor vessel measuring neutron flux for reactor power monitoring.
Sensor measuring core exit temperature for core cooling monitoring.
Instrument measuring primary coolant flow rate.
Instrument measuring liquid level in the pressurizer.
Instrument monitoring pressure inside the containment building.
System of detectors monitoring radiation levels in various plant areas and effluent streams.
Device used to safely recombine hydrogen and oxygen in containment following an accident.
Fan-coil unit used to cool containment air during normal operation and accidents.
Pump circulating chilled water to various HVAC cooling coils.
Compressor supplying clean, dry air for pneumatically operated instruments and valves.
Compressor supplying compressed air for general plant services and maintenance.
Switchgear distributing power to large motors like reactor coolant pumps and safety injection pumps.
Assembly controlling power to low voltage motors and equipment.
Bank of batteries providing highly reliable DC power for control systems and critical instrumentation.
Device converting DC power from batteries into highly reliable AC power for vital instruments.
